data.title: High-Stakes Bets Placed on Rockets vs. Lakers NBA Playoff Game
data.description:
In a significant development in the NBA playoffs, a notable account with a winning rate exceeding 58% has invested $110,000 on the Houston Rockets defeating the Los Angeles Lakers, according to Odaily. The average opening price for this bet was 64.4¢. Additionally, the account purchased $13,000 on the Rockets winning with a 4.5-point spread against the Lakers.

The playoff game between the Rockets and the Lakers is scheduled to begin at 8:30 PM UTC+8. The Lakers will be without players Luka Dončić and Austin Rivers, while the Rockets will miss Fred VanVleet and Steven Adams. Furthermore, renowned NBA journalist Shams has reported that Rockets star Kevin Durant will also be absent from the game.

data.title: The market capitalization of ASTEROID, a Meme coin on the Ethereum blockchain, has surpassed $130 million, with a 24-hour increase exceeding 400%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of the Ethereum blockchain's Meme coin ASTEROID has surged past $130 million, reaching a high of $164 million, and is currently trading at $130.8 million, representing a 24-hour increase of over 400%. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks. Meme coins generally lack real-world application scenarios and rely more on speculative hype; caution is advised when entering the market.

data.title: ETH Chain Meme Coin ASTEROID Surges Over 400% in 24 Hours
data.description:
The market capitalization of the ETH chain meme coin ASTEROID has surged past $130 million, reaching a peak of $164 million before settling at $130.8 million. According to Odaily, the coin experienced a 24-hour increase of over 400%.

Odaily advises investors to be cautious due to the high volatility of meme coin prices. These coins often lack practical applications and are primarily driven by speculative sentiment.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Faces Skepticism Over Optimistic Negotiation Claims
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed optimism about ongoing negotiations, suggesting that a breakthrough is still possible. According to BlockBeats, this assertion is increasingly questioned, particularly as Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz again after a brief reopening.

Sinan Al-Azodi, Director of Middle East Studies at George Washington University, voiced skepticism about Trump's statements, cautioning that the situation on the ground is quite different. "I'm not sure I can believe President Trump's claims are true. The President often makes assertions that are not necessarily accurate," Al-Azodi remarked.

He cited Trump's claim that Iranians would abandon their enriched uranium, which Iran has completely denied. Al-Azodi believes Iran's reclosure of the strait is a response to the U.S.'s "double blockade," recognizing the Strait of Hormuz as Iran's "biggest bargaining chip."

data.title: Japan's Yen Faces Pressure Amid Inflation Concerns, Says ADB President
data.description:
On April 19, Masato Kanda, President of the Asian Development Bank, expressed concerns that the Japanese yen might face further pressure if the market perceives the Bank of Japan's actions against inflation risks as too slow. According to Jin10, Kanda, who previously served as Japan's top foreign exchange diplomat, stated on Friday evening that investors tend to buy dollars during global tensions partly because the U.S. is an oil exporter. However, even if these positions are unwound, the yen is unlikely to rise significantly against the dollar.

Kanda highlighted that the primary reason for this is the interest rate differential. With the market closely monitoring potential actions by the U.S. Federal Reserve, many believe that if the Bank of Japan lags in addressing inflation risks, the yen will be left behind. During the International Monetary Fund and World Bank Group meetings in Washington this week, Kanda also noted that concerns over Japan's fiscal sustainability could lead investors to sell the yen.

data.title: House Draft Proposes Exclusion of Gains on Regulated Stablecoins
data.description: A bipartisan discussion draft in the House suggests excluding gains and losses from the sale of regulated payment stablecoins, provided the holder's basis does not drop below 99% of the redemption value. According to NS3.AI, the proposal specifically targets US dollar-pegged tokens issued under the GENIUS Act framework, while excluding brokers and dealers from this carve-out. It is important to note that the draft is not yet law, and no issuer has been granted permitted payment stablecoin issuer status.

data.title: Kelp DAO's rsETH bridging protocol was suspected of being hacked, resulting in a loss of approximately $292 million.
data.description: On-chain data shows that Kelp DAO's LayerZero-based rsETH bridging protocol was suspected of being exploited by hackers, resulting in the loss of 116,500 rsETH, worth approximately $292 million. Kelp DAO officials stated that they have discovered suspicious cross-chain activity involving rsETH, and have suspended rsETH contracts on the mainnet and multiple Layer 2 platforms. They are currently working with security experts and will provide updates as they become available.

data.title: Aave is suspected of incurring bad debts due to the rsETH attack; the exact amount is currently unknown.
data.description: Aave is suspected of incurring bad debts due to Kelp DAO attackers using stolen rsETH as collateral to borrow ETH, with the exact amount currently unknown. Aave officials stated, "The rsETH market on Aave V3 and Aave V4 has been frozen. We are reviewing rsETH borrowing information that occurred on Aave after the attack and will share more details as soon as possible. If the protocol has accumulated bad debts as a result of this incident, we will explore ways to mitigate the losses." OKX market data shows AAVE currently trading at 105.15 USDT, down 9.56% in the last 24 hours.

data.title: Strategy Targets Reduced Volatility and Consistent Bitcoin Acquisition
data.description: A new strategy has been introduced with the goal of minimizing market volatility while ensuring regular bitcoin purchases. According to CoinDesk, this approach also aims to establish the first bi-monthly paying preferred shares available in the market. By focusing on consistent acquisition, the strategy seeks to provide stability and predictability for investors interested in bitcoin. This initiative is designed to appeal to those looking for a reliable investment option in the often unpredictable cryptocurrency market. The introduction of bi-monthly paying preferred shares is a unique feature, setting this strategy apart from others in the financial sector. This development reflects a growing trend towards creating more structured and dependable investment opportunities within the digital currency space. As the market continues to evolve, such strategies may become increasingly important for investors seeking to navigate the complexities of cryptocurrency investments.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Decreases Amid Record BTC Sales by Public Miners
data.description:
Bitcoin mining difficulty experienced a decline on Saturday, reflecting the ongoing challenges faced by the industry. According to Cointelegraph, the difficulty level dropped to approximately 135.5 T, marking a modest decrease of about 1.1% over the past 24 hours, as reported by CoinWarz. Despite this decrease, mining difficulty is expected to rise in the next adjustment period. CoinWarz projects that the next adjustment will occur on May 01, 2026, at 01:24:54 PM UTC, increasing the difficulty from 135.59 T to 137.43 T, which will happen in 1,865 blocks, approximately 12 days, 18 hours, and 41 minutes from now.

Bitcoin miners have been grappling with significant challenges over the past year, including reduced block rewards, escalating energy prices, a prolonged crypto bear market, and geopolitical disruptions. These factors have created economic headwinds for miners, prompting publicly traded Bitcoin mining companies to sell record amounts of BTC to cover operating expenses. In the first quarter of 2026, these companies sold more BTC than in all four quarters of 2025 combined, according to TheEnergyMag. Companies such as MARA, CleanSpark, Riot, Cango, Core Scientific, and Bitdeer collectively sold over 32,000 BTC during Q1 2026. This figure surpasses the 20,000 BTC sold in Q2 2022, coinciding with the collapse of the Terra-Luna ecosystem, which triggered an extended bear market.

Miners often sell their BTC to cover operating expenses, which are denominated in fiat currency. However, as the cost of mining a single BTC surpasses spot market prices, many BTC mining companies are struggling to remain profitable. According to CoinShares' Q1 2026 mining report, up to 20% of Bitcoin miners are currently unprofitable under existing economic conditions. The report highlights that Q4 2025 was the most challenging quarter for Bitcoin miners since the April 2024 halving. The authors attribute this to the sharp BTC correction in October 2025, which saw BTC's price plummet from a high of about $125,000 to approximately $86,000 by December 2025, coupled with the increasing computational difficulty of adding blocks, as significant obstacles for the mining industry.

data.title: UK FCA Proposes Crypto Regulation Draft Targeting Stablecoins and Trading Platforms
data.description: The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has unveiled a proposal for crypto regulation, aiming to address stablecoins, staking, and trading platforms. According to NS3.AI, this draft is a precursor to a more comprehensive bill expected by October 2027. The consultation period for the proposal is open until June. Bill Hughes from Consensys noted that the draft's language might require many crypto businesses to seek FCA authorization.

data.title: GalaxyOne Focuses on Staking Products for Retail Investors
data.description: GalaxyOne is shifting its focus towards staking products rather than prediction markets for retail investors with investible assets ranging from $100,000 to $1 million. According to NS3.AI, Zac Prince highlighted that the platform started supporting Solana staking last month. Additionally, there are plans to introduce Ethereum staking and lending options in the future.

data.title: Iran's Crypto Transactions: $3 Billion Shift Since 2022, BTC Accepted for Oil Ship Tolls
data.description: Iran has reportedly begun accepting Bitcoin (BTC) as a payment method for oil ship tolls through the Strait of Hormuz, according to Sam Lyman of the Bitcoin Policy Institute. Since 2022, Iran has shifted approximately $3 billion in cryptocurrency transactions. According to NS3.AI, most of these transactions are still denominated in USDt. However, Lyman noted that there is currently no on-chain evidence of any BTC toll payments.

data.title: USDT Transfer: $285 Million Moved from Aave to Spark
data.description: A significant transfer of 285,111,406 USDT, valued at approximately $285 million, has been executed from Aave to Spark. According to NS3.AI, this transaction highlights the ongoing shifts within the cryptocurrency market, reflecting strategic movements by investors and platforms. The transfer underscores the dynamic nature of digital asset management and the evolving landscape of decentralized finance.

data.title: Credit Investors Shift to Riskier Debt Amid Iran-U.S. Truce Hopes
data.description: Credit investors are increasingly turning to riskier debt, anticipating that the truce between Iran and the United States will continue. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this shift as investors move away from safer assets they have preferred since the conflict began in late February. This trend reflects growing confidence in the stability of the geopolitical situation, encouraging investors to seek higher returns in more volatile markets.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 630 USDT with a 1.63% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 18, 2026, 18:43 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 630 USDT and is now trading at 629.72998 USDT, with a narrowed 1.63% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: AI TRENDS | AI Hardware Market Projections Boost AI Crypto Tokens
data.description: Jensen Huang's recent remarks on the potential of a $1 trillion AI hardware market by 2027 have reignited interest in AI-related cryptocurrencies. According to NS3.AI, this development has positively impacted tokens like Bittensor's TAO and Fetch.ai's FET. In response, Grayscale has increased its allocation of TAO to approximately 43% within its decentralized AI fund this week.

data.title: Parasite Pool Mines Second Bitcoin Block After 48 Days
data.description:
Parasite Pool has successfully mined its second Bitcoin block, number 945,601, on Friday. According to CoinDesk, this achievement comes approximately 48 days after the pool's initial block discovery. Parasite Pool operates by rewarding the block finder with 1 BTC, while the remaining rewards are distributed among all participants. This unique reward structure aims to incentivize miners by offering a guaranteed payout to the block finder, while also ensuring that all contributors receive a share of the earnings. The pool's approach has garnered attention within the cryptocurrency community for its innovative distribution model.

The mining of block 945,601 marks a significant milestone for Parasite Pool, highlighting its growing presence in the Bitcoin mining landscape. The pool's ability to mine a second block within a relatively short period demonstrates its potential to compete with more established mining operations. As the cryptocurrency market continues to evolve, mining pools like Parasite Pool play a crucial role in maintaining the decentralized nature of Bitcoin by enabling smaller miners to participate and earn rewards. This development underscores the dynamic and competitive nature of the Bitcoin mining industry, where new players are constantly emerging and challenging the status quo.

data.title: Hyperliquid Founder Reflects on Prediction Market Project Challenges
data.description: Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan discussed the challenges faced by his prediction market project launched in 2018. According to NS3.AI, Yan identified product-market fit as a significant hurdle, compounded by the transition from a bull to a bear market, which diminished users' interest in exploring on-chain products.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's Crypto Holdings and Potential Impact on Federal Reserve Policies
data.description: Kevin Warsh, a candidate for the Federal Reserve chair, has revealed his crypto-related investments, including holdings in Polymarket. According to NS3.AI, Warsh has committed to divesting these assets if confirmed for the position. Reuters highlighted Warsh's preference for a reduced Federal Reserve balance sheet and a more stringent monetary policy, which could have implications for Bitcoin and the accessibility of crypto banks.
